title: Today's AI Brief: Meta’s AI Agent for WhatsApp Business Goes Global
summary: Meta's AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now global, and a Māori text-to-speech model challenges big tech values.
imageAlt: Image from source article for Meta’s AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
imageCaption: Meta’s AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
imageSourceTitle: Meta’s AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and I'm excited to share some fresh AI news with you. Let's dive into the headlines.

First up, Meta's AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally. This is a big deal because WhatsApp has been a go-to communication platform for businesses of all sizes. By infusing AI into the mix, Meta aims to turn WhatsApp into a workflow software for small and medium-sized businesses. Think of it like having a virtual assistant that can help with customer inquiries, bookings, and more, all within the WhatsApp interface.

Next, a Māori text-to-speech model is making waves by challenging big tech's values. This model is part of a larger effort to create more inclusive and diverse AI systems. The Māori language, one of New Zealand's official languages, is the focus here. It's fascinating to see how this model is being developed with a deep respect for the language and culture, offering a unique perspective on AI development.

I'm looking at these developments and wondering about the implications for AI adoption and data boundaries. As AI becomes more integrated into our daily tools, like WhatsApp, it's clear that the lines between personal and business communications are getting blurrier. How these platforms manage data and ensure safety will be crucial for user trust.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
